description: ClickHouse Cloud managed Postgres — fleet overview scraped from /v1/organizations/.../postgres/prometheus.

panels.title: Services
panels.description: Current state and capacity of all Postgres services. Disk used % shaded yellow ≥85, red ≥90. Join key is the postgres_service UUID.

panels.title: CPU utilization (% of allocated cores)
panels.description: Non-idle CPU seconds divided by allocated cores, expressed as a percentage. Values >100% indicate sample aliasing or burst behavior.

CPU time per mode (user, system, iowait, steal, etc.) as % of allocated cores, stacked. Most useful filtered to a single service — the stack tells you whether CPU is going to application code, kernel, disk waits, or hypervisor contention.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (postgres_service_name, mode) (rate(PostgresServer_CPUSeconds_Total{mode!="idle", postgres_service_name=~"$service"}[$__rate_interval])) / on(postgres_service_name) group_left() PostgresServer_CPUCores

panels.title: CPU by mode
Memory used %
panels.description: Total memory in use as a percentage of the service's memory limit.

panels.title: Memory cache %
panels.description: Memory used by buffers and cache as a percentage of total memory. High values are normal under sustained read load.

panels.title: Buffer cache hit ratio
panels.description: Buffer cache hit ratio — blocks served from cache vs total blocks accessed. Drops indicate a cold cache or working set exceeding RAM.

panels.title: Active connections (by state)
panels.description: Active connections per service, broken out by state (active, idle). With one service selected, this shows that service's connection mix over time.

panels.title: Disk utilization % over time
panels.description: Filesystem space used as % of allocated storage. Threshold lines at 85% (yellow) and 90% (red).

panels.title: Transactions committed / sec
panels.description: Rate of committed transactions per second.

panels.title: Transactions rolled back / sec
panels.description: Rate of rolled-back transactions per second. Spikes here without a corresponding rise in the ratio mean traffic also rose.

panels.title: Rollback ratio %
panels.description: Rollbacks as a fraction of total transactions (commits + rollbacks). clamp_min prevents divide-by-zero — idle services report 0%.

panels.title: Tuples fetched / sec
panels.description: Rows returned to clients per second.

panels.title: Tuples written / sec (insert + update + delete)
panels.description: Rows written per second, split by operation (insert, update, delete).

panels.title: Network bytes / sec (rx up, tx down)
panels.description: Network bytes per second. Receive (rx) is shown positive, transmit (tx) negative for visual contrast on one axis.

panels.title: Disk ops / sec (reads up, writes down)
panels.description: Filesystem operations per second. Reads shown positive, writes negative for visual contrast on one axis.
On-disk size per database over time. With many services selected this gets crowded — filter via the Service variable, or sort the legend table to find the biggest databases.
panels.title: Database sizes over time
Deadlocks / sec
panels.description: Deadlocks detected per second. Any non-zero value warrants investigation.

title: ClickHouse Cloud — Postgres Services
